title: The Souler Opposite
review_summary: An original love story with perfectly-timed humor, this movie delivers both laughs and heartfelt messages about overcoming adversity through comedic storytelling; Chris Meloni's performance as Barry Singer deserves Oscar recognition.

title: Reckless
review_summary: The reviewer enjoys this lighthearted mini-series about romantic complications between an older woman and younger man despite some clichés; Robson Greene shines in his role as the devoted lover facing desire's ebb and flow—suggesting it could have been tighter with only two episodes. A sequel is expected soon on Netflix, though they feel a second installment might not be necessary for this story.

title: Stanley & Iris
review_summary: Fair drama/love story with decent acting but criticized cinematography, screenplay, directing, editing; average script that may appeal to fans of Fonda/Diaz. Focuses on middle-aged cohabitation and touches upon themes like literacy.

title: I.Q.
review_summary: A charming romantic comedy with witty performances by Walter Matthau as Albert Einstein and Meg Ryan's Catherine impresses through universal appeal despite its light-heartedness; subtly explores themes like destiny versus accident.

title: If You Only Knew
review_summary: The reviewer enjoyed "old-fashioned" romantic storyline; outcome of romance revealed in film. Sufficient for those seeking classic love tale with twists.

title: I.Q.
review_summary: A heartfelt, quote-filled chick flick romance where Walter M's portrayal of Einstein shines; highly enjoyable for repeat viewings.

title: Titanic
review_summary: The reviewer fondly recalls watching 'Titanic' as a child at 12 years old when it was released; however, upon rewatching for themselves now (at age 14), they are unequivocally convinced of its status as the best movie ever made. Despite acknowledging it's subjective to personal taste and that not all movies can achieve this level, Titanic excels in romance, humor, disaster themes
